R to @NetramDefence: More widely, the UAE and India share a deep and enduring relationship underpinned by a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership that has expanded significantly in recent years across trade, energy and infrastructure. 

In 2022, the UAE and India signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement, which has led to a 14.7 % increase in trade activity in 2025 according to data from the UAE government. 

These developments build on the momentum generated by earlier agreements and growing economic integration between the two countries. Bilateral ties have strengthened significantly in recent years, supported by major investments—including a $5 billion UAE investment package and long-term energy contracts, with both countries targeting $200 billion in trade by 2032.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: A key feature of this collaboration is the “shared reserve model,” wherein Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) stores crude oil in India’s SPR facilities while retaining ownership, but India has the first right to access these reserves during emergencies.

 This mechanism allows India to strengthen its strategic reserves without fully bearing procurement costs. The agreement also expands storage beyond Mangalore to include Visakhapatnam and planned facilities at Chandikhol in Odisha, while exploring storage in the UAE itself, including Fujairah. 

In parallel, ADNOC has expanded its engagement with Indian Oil Corporation to explore long-term LPG supply and trading opportunities, building on an existing term contract since 2023.
tweets.content:
A key feature of this collaboration is the “shared reserve model,” wherein Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) stores crude oil in India’s SPR facilities while retaining ownership, but India has the first right to access these reserves during emergencies.

This mechanism allows India to strengthen its strategic reserves without fully bearing procurement costs. The agreement also expands storage beyond Mangalore to include Visakhapatnam and planned facilities at Chandikhol in Odisha, while exploring storage in the UAE itself, including Fujairah.

In parallel, ADNOC has expanded its engagement with Indian Oil Corporation to explore long-term LPG supply and trading opportunities, building on an existing term contract since 2023.

tweets.content:

Additionally, the two countries established a MoU regarding strategic petroleum reserves, which will enhance the UAE's participation in India’s Strategic Petroleum Reserves (SPR) to a total of up to 30 million barrels.

RT by @OsintTV: प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी की सुरक्षा में कटौती पर पूर्व रॉ प्रमुख सामंत गोयल की कड़ी चेतावनी: “ड्रोन, स्नाइपर और वैश्विक आतंकी खतरों के इस दौर में प्रधानमंत्री की सुरक्षा घटाना राष्ट्रहित में नहीं”, बोले— 25 वर्षों से मोदी आतंकी संगठनों के निशाने पर, सुरक्षा और मजबूत करने की जरूरत

Former R&AW Secretary Samant Goel has strongly opposed any reduction in Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s security convoy, warning that the current global security environment is more dangerous and unpredictable than ever before.

Goel emphasized that security is never a status symbol—it is determined solely by threat assessment. He noted that PM Modi has remained under threat for over 25 years, beginning from his tenure as Chief Minister of Gujarat, and that his firm stance on national security makes him a constant target for hostile elements, including jihadi groups and adversarial state-backed networks.

Citing multiple assassination attempts on US President Donald Trump despite America’s extensive security apparatus, Goel argued that India should strengthen, not downsize, the Prime Minister’s protection. He highlighted emerging threats such as armed drones, long-range sniper weapons, and lone-wolf attackers, all of which require enhanced and adaptive security protocols.

According to Goel, reducing the convoy could make it easier for adversaries to identify which vehicle carries the Prime Minister. He also pointed out that foreign governments often calibrate their own security arrangements for visiting leaders based on the standards maintained in their home countries.

His message was unequivocal: the Prime Minister’s security is not about optics or symbolism. It is a core national security priority that safeguards India’s leadership continuity, decision-making capability, and strategic stability.
